summary: 17th-century French Baroque painter known for candlelit chiaroscuro scenes. Known for The Penitent Magdalene (woman with skull reflecting candle in mirror), The Cheat with the Ace of Diamonds/Clubs, The Fortune Teller, Joseph the Carpenter. Influenced by Caravaggio. Based in Luneville.

works.name: The Penitent Magdalene
works.description: Woman with skull on lap staring into mirror reflecting candlelight. Also Magdalene with the Smoking Flame (similar scene with smoking flame). Wikipedia

clues.clue: Woman sitting with skull on lap, staring into mirror reflecting candle light; also Magdalene with the Smoking Flame; appeared in The Little Mermaid (Ariel's grotto)
examples: seated woman clasping her hands above a skull and looking into the reflection of a candle in the mirror
La Tour's painting of this woman 'with the smoking flame' can be seen in Ariel's grotto in a scene from The Little Mermaid

works.name: The Cheat (two versions)
works.description: Cheat with Ace of Diamonds and Cheat with Ace of Clubs. Three people collude against a foppish youth; card sharp retrieves card from belt. Shifty woman in feathered hat. Wikipedia

images.caption: The Cheat with the Ace of Diamonds

clues.clue: Three people collude against foppish youth; card sharp retrieves card from belt; shifty woman in feathered hat; two versions (Ace of Diamonds, Ace of Clubs)
examples: A foppish young man on the right sits next to a courtesan and has wine poured for him as his opponent cheats
a shifty woman in a feathered hat across from a card sharp retrieving a card from his belt

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Based in Luneville, patronized by Louis XIII. Works largely forgotten until 1915 article by Herman Voss. Influenced by Caravaggio and the Caravaggisti (Gerrit van Honthorst). Other works: The Fortune Teller (boy's purse and watch stolen), Joseph the Carpenter, The Musician's Brawl/Quarrel (hurdy-gurdy players fighting), St. Sebastian Tended by St. Irene, The Newborn Christ, The Flea Catcher. Fortune Teller at center of forgery controversy at Met.

clues.clue: Candle-lit interior scenes; chiaroscuro influenced by Caravaggio and Caravaggisti (especially Gerrit van Honthorst)
examples: this French imitator of Caravaggio known for his interior scenes lit only by candles
La Tour was heavily influenced by the tenebrism seen in paintings by this Italian artist

clues.clue: Based in Luneville; patronized by Louis XIII
examples: did most of his work in the small village of Luneville under the patronage of Louis XIII

clues.clue: Works largely forgotten until 1915 article by German art historian Herman Voss
examples: the publication of a 1915 article by German art historian Herman Voss

clues.clue: The Fortune Teller: boy has coin purse and watch stolen while his fortune is read; center of forgery controversy when Met bought it
examples: He painted a boy having his fortune read while two women surreptitiously steal his coin purse and his watch

clues.clue: The Musician's Brawl/Quarrel: hurdy-gurdy player fights; one squeezes lemon juice into blind beggar's eye
examples: a musician squeezing lemon juice into the eye of a supposedly blind fellow beggar
a man holding a knife at bay with a woodwind instrument

clues.clue: Many paintings of hurdy-gurdy players; attributed to mysterious 'Hurdy-Gurdy Master'
examples: Many paintings that may be by this man have been attributed to the mysterious Hurdy-Gurdy master

comprehensive_summary:
Georges de la Tour was a 17th-century French Baroque painter based in Luneville, patronized by Louis XIII, known for chiaroscuro interior scenes lit by a single candle. His works were largely forgotten until a 1915 article by Herman Voss.

The Penitent Magdalene shows a woman with a skull on her lap staring into a mirror reflecting candlelight (the 'Smoking Flame' version appeared in The Little Mermaid). The Cheat exists in two versions (Ace of Diamonds and Ace of Clubs), showing three people colluding against a foppish youth as a card sharp retrieves a card from his belt.

The Fortune Teller shows a boy having his purse and watch stolen while his fortune is read (center of a forgery controversy at the Met). The Musician's Brawl features hurdy-gurdy players fighting, with one squeezing lemon juice into a beggar's eye. Other works include Joseph the Carpenter, St. Sebastian Tended by St. Irene, The Newborn Christ, and The Flea Catcher. He was heavily influenced by Caravaggio and the Caravaggisti, especially Gerrit van Honthorst.
